channel.title: Cortex
channel.description: A reading list that writes itself, and learns what you know.

item.title: Every Channel Decays — The Manual Work That Buys You Time First
item.description: Every acquisition channel decays from the day you find it. The founders who last are the ones who do the unscalable, manual work first, and use it to build the taste that picks the next channel well.

item.title: How Embeddings Make 'Relevant' a Computable Thing
item.description: Retrieval-augmented generation depends on one mechanism to decide what counts as relevant: turning meaning into vectors you can measure the distance between. Here's how that works, and where it lives in a Next.js/Supabase stack.

item.title: Aperture, ISO, and the Shutter Speed Rule Unique to Video
item.description: The three controls that expose every shot — and the one rule, specific to video, that photography tutorials rarely mention.

item.title: The US Jobs Miss That's Quietly Repricing Australian Mortgage Rates
item.description: US payrolls badly missed forecasts overnight and Fed rate-hike odds collapsed — a shift that reaches Australian bank funding costs before the RBA does anything at all.

item.title: The Third Interstellar Comet Turns Out to Be 12 Billion Years Old
item.description: New James Webb spectroscopy on 3I/Atlas, only the third confirmed interstellar visitor, shows chemistry that doesn't match anything born in our solar system, and puts its age at up to 12 billion years.

item.title: What Actually Speeds Up Recovery From a Bad Ankle Sprain
item.description: The three-grade system, why RICE alone is outdated, and the return-to-play numbers that matter more than how many weeks have passed since the injury.

item.title: Colour Theory for Interfaces: Picking a Palette That Actually Works
item.description: Visual hierarchy told you what to make loud. Colour is how you decide which loud thing means what. Hue/saturation/lightness, harmony schemes, and the semantic-colour rule most beginner palettes break.

item.title: Negative Gearing Reform Is Law — What the Carve-Outs Actually Change
item.description: The negative gearing and CGT reform has passed — then the government announced small-business carve-outs and denied it's a backdown. What's actually different, what isn't, and what to check before you act.

item.title: The Jagged Frontier: Why Your AI Feature Needs an Eval, Not Just a Vibe Check
item.description: AI capability is uneven across domains and tasks — what works brilliantly in one context fails unpredictably in another. Here's how to build a lightweight eval set so you know your AI feature actually works, instead of hoping it does.

item.title: The Life Insurance Code Gets 85 New Teeth — Kell's Final Report Lands
item.description: Peter Kell's final review of the Life Insurance Code of Practice lands with 85 recommendations — up from 72 in April — centred on mental health claims handling, plus same-day evidence that code governance committees are actively enforcing, not just writing rules.

item.title: Broker Brief: End of FY26 — Tax Reform Fallout, APRA's Presale Shakeup, SME Arrears
item.description: Three developments every AU broker needs to track as the financial year closes: lenders revising servicing calculators after Labor's investor tax reforms, APRA proposing new presale rules for development finance, and SME payment arrears on the rise.

item.title: Your First 10 Users: The Only Number That Matters at Launch
item.description: Getting early users for a consumer app is less about marketing and more about manual, targeted outreach — and in an AI world, taste is the skill that separates winners.

item.title: LRBA ban, CGT reform, rate split: the broker brief for June 2026
item.description: Parliament passes negative gearing and CGT changes, APRA signals the end of SMSF residential lending, and major banks disagree on where rates go next. Here's what each shift means for your client conversations.

item.title: The four ways indie apps make money — and how to pick yours
item.description: Freemium, subscription, one-time, and marketplace aren't interchangeable — each fits a specific usage pattern and buyer psychology. Get this right early and you stop building for users who never pay.

item.title: Visual Hierarchy: Making the Eye Land Where You Want
item.description: The single skill that separates a polished interface from a homemade one. Four levers — size, weight, contrast, and space — that tell a reader what to look at first, and how to test it in seconds.

item.title: How to read APRA's quarterly statistics
item.description: APRA publishes a quarterly statistical suite covering superannuation, life insurance, general insurance and private health. Here's what each set of numbers actually tells a practitioner — and what signals to watch.

item.title: Sleep Science for Recovery: What the Evidence Actually Says
item.description: Sleep is the recovery layer that makes training stick. Covers sleep architecture, the U-shaped dose-response curve, temperature, nutrition, magnesium, and the myths worth ditching.

item.title: The Infrastructure Bill Behind AI: Why Your Tech Is Getting More Expensive
item.description: AI isn't just a software upgrade — it's a trillion-dollar infrastructure build that's landing on consumer price tags. Here's the cost chain from data centres to your next MacBook.

item.title: The RBA's Tightening Cycle: Three Hikes In, What Comes Next?
item.description: The Reserve Bank has raised the cash rate for a third consecutive meeting. What's driving it, how it flows through to your mortgage and super, and when the market thinks rates might finally fall.

item.title: The IFRS 17 CSM, in plain terms
item.description: The contractual service margin is how IFRS 17 stops insurers booking profit on day one — and spreads it over the life of the cover instead.

item.title: What rising real yields mean for your portfolio
item.description: Real yields are the interest rate after inflation — and when they rise, almost every asset you own has to re-price.

item.title: Why retrieval beats fine-tuning for most apps
item.description: When you need an LLM to know your data, reach for retrieval before fine-tuning — it is cheaper, fresher, and easier to debug.

item.title: Zone 2 training, explained
item.description: The slow, conversational pace that builds your aerobic base — what Zone 2 is, why it works, and how to find yours.
